title: NCC Roles by Patient Type: Primary, Co-Managed, Consult

objectives.outcome: Primary, Co-managed, Consult roles and authority scope
Typical diagnoses for each role at BNI/SJHMC
What you can order, what to coordinate, what is advisory
Gray zones: emergent deteriorations, family meetings

roles.scope: Direct overall care, perform procedures, place orders, consult others, discharge from ICU
Routine orders within scope; large management decisions require coordination with primary; family meetings shared
Recommendations to primary; orders and family meetings only with primary's expressed permission

table.docs: H&P, daily progress, transfer/discharge, code status, family-meeting notes
Daily co-management progress note parallel to primary
Consult note with recommendations + active follow-up notes

didactic_sections.heading: NCC Primary Diagnoses
list: Autoimmune encephalitis, demyelinating conditions requiring ICU

list.diagnosis: AIS at high risk for decompensation
Cardiac arrest with anoxic brain injury

didactic_sections.heading: Emergent Gray Zones
key_points: Acute decompensation on a consult patient — act in patient's best interest
Notify primary team and NCC attending in parallel
Reset boundaries when primary is at bedside

case_vignettes.correct_action: Initiate nicardipine per ICH bundle; notify stroke attending; coordinate surgical referral if expansion

case_vignettes.stem: 22 y/o severe TBI, ICP 28, TICU attending elsewhere
case_vignettes.correct_action: Treat ICP per protocol; document urgency; notify TICU and NCC attending in parallel; reset on TICU arrival

case_vignettes.stem: 67 y/o myasthenic crisis on PLEX, husband asks 'should we keep going?'
case_vignettes.correct_action: Sit down, answer, document; schedule formal family meeting if needed

linked_protocols: Admission Diagnosis Guidelines for NCC Pod 4NNC
nursing_considerations: On consult patients, communicate clearly to bedside RN that recommendations require primary team order entry
On co-managed patients, designate who is paging RN in the morning
pharm_considerations: On consult patients, pharmacy will route drug-interaction concerns to the primary team unless NCC has been delegated specific authority

visuals.subject: Primary / Co-managed / Consult flowchart by diagnosis
visuals.notes: Tap a diagnosis to see role and primary team

visuals.subject: Emergent gray-zone decision rules
visuals.notes: Act, document time-pressure, notify in parallel

assessment.item: Identify primary vs. NCC role for severe TBI with ICP monitor
Pre-order escalation for induced HTN in SAH co-managed patient
Handling of family prognosis question on consult patient
Matrix recall: vasopressor initiation by role
